title: CAAS (Amendment) Bill introduced in Parliament to Implement Sustainable Aviation Fuel
content:
On September 22, 2025, the Ministry of Transport introduced the Civil Aviation Authority of Singapore (CAAS) (Amendment) Bill in Parliament, proposing legislative changes to enable the implementation of Sustainable Aviation Fuel (SAF) policies. If passed, the Bill will empower CAAS to collect a SAF Levy from flights departing Singapore, establish a dedicated SAF Fund to manage these collections, and centralize the procurement and allocation of SAF and its environmental attributes.

The SAF Levy, which will begin in 2026, is designed as a fixed quantum to provide cost certainty for air transport users. This initiative aligns with Singaporeâ€™s Sustainable Air Hub Blueprint, which sets a mandatory 1% SAF target from 2026, with a goal to increase this to 3-5% by 2030.

This legislation is a key part of Singaporeâ€™s broader strategy to decarbonize its aviation sector. The blueprint outlines a plan to reduce domestic aviation emissions by 20% by 2030 and achieve net-zero emissions for both domestic and international aviation by 2050. The use of SAF is considered a critical pathway, projected to contribute approximately 65% of the carbon emission reductions needed to meet the 2050 target.
author: Analysts Account
